QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_6

question.text: In a close-ended mutual fund scheme, when can investors typically buy units?

answer.text: Only during the New Fund Offer (NFO) period
On any business day throughout the year
Only on the first day of every month
Only when the NAV falls below Rs. 10

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A close-ended scheme has a fixed maturity period (for example, 3 years or 5 years). Investors can buy units only during the initial New Fund Offer (NFO) period. After the NFO closes, new purchases directly from the fund are not allowed — though units may be listed on a stock exchange for trading. **The Trap:** The ability to buy on any business day is a feature of open-ended schemes, not close-ended. Fixed Maturity Plans (FMPs) are a common example of close-ended schemes. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under scheme types. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember that as a new cadre distributor, Fixed Maturity Plans (FMPs) are one of your permitted products — and FMPs are close-ended. Make sure clients understand they cannot exit before maturity easily.
